nodes.context: Outlaw motorcycle club involved in drug trafficking, money laundering, robbery, extortion, firearms violations, murder, and assault. Operates internationally with chapters in the US, Australia, Germany, Netherlands, and New Zealand.
Australian outlaw motorcycle club that was largely patched over by the Mongols in 2013. Involved in a turf war with the Mongols in Queensland after 2020.
Lebanese-origin crime syndicate in Bremen, Germany, with over 10,000 members. Founded the German chapter of the Mongols MC in 2010.
Major outlaw motorcycle club, rival of the Mongols in the US and involved in conflicts in Germany and the Netherlands.
New Zealand street gang involved in a turf war with the Mongols in the Bay of Plenty in 2020.
Prison gang and organized crime syndicate allied with the Mongols in the US.
Southern California street gang coalition allied with the Mexican Mafia and the Mongols.
Prison gang and organized crime syndicate allied with the Mongols in Northern California.
Northern California street gang coalition allied with Nuestra Familia and the Mongols.
White supremacist prison gang allied with the Mongols in the US.
Mexican drug cartel that supplies the Mongols with cocaine for distribution in Los Angeles.
Outlaw motorcycle club, rival of the Mongols in the US.
Motorcycle club considered a rival of the Mongols in the US.
Outlaw motorcycle club, rival of the Mongols in the US but allied with them in Tucson in 1982.
Outlaw motorcycle club, rival of the Mongols in the US.
Motorcycle club that had a war with the Mongols over prostitution rackets in Tucson in 1982.
Motorcycle club involved with the Mongols and Hells Angels in a sexual assault incident in 1972.
Motorcycle club involved with the Mongols and Hells Angels in a sexual assault incident in 1972.
Large street gang in Los Angeles; some members recruited by Mongols.
Street gang in Los Angeles; some members recruited by Mongols.
Street gang involved in a narcotics transaction with Mongols in 2004.
Sureño gang involved in a shooting with Mongols in 2004.
Outlaw motorcycle club; listed as ally of Mongols in infobox.

edges.context: In 2013, the Mongols patched over approximately 90% of the Finks' members, making it the largest motorcycle club amalgamation in Australia.
After the 2020 assassination of Shane Bowden, a turf war broke out between the Mongols and Finks in Queensland, involving firebombings and bashings.
The German chapter of the Mongols in Bremen was founded by members of the Miri-Clan, a Lebanese crime syndicate.
The Mongols and Hells Angels are long-standing rivals in the US, with conflicts including a mass brawl in 1977. They also clashed in the Netherlands in 2016.
In January 2020, the Mongols were involved in a turf war with the Mongrel Mob in the Bay of Plenty, New Zealand.
The Mongols are allied with the Mexican Mafia in the US, particularly in Southern California.
The Mongols are allied with the Sureños, a Southern California street gang coalition, as part of the Mexican Mafia–Sureño coalition.
The Mongols are allied with Nuestra Familia in Northern California.
The Mongols are allied with the Norteños, a Northern California street gang coalition, as part of the Nuestra Familia–Norteño alliance.
The Mongols are allied with the Nazi Lowriders, a white supremacist prison gang.
The Tijuana Cartel supplies the Mongols with cocaine for wholesale distribution in the Los Angeles area.
The Bandidos are listed as a rival gang of the Mongols in the US.
The Iron Order is listed as a rival gang of the Mongols in the US.
The Outlaws are listed as a rival gang of the Mongols in the US, but they were allied in Tucson in 1982.
The Vagos are listed as a rival gang of the Mongols in the US.
The Mongols had a war with the Dirty Dozen over prostitution rackets in Tucson in 1982.
In 1982, the Mongols aligned with the Outlaws in Tucson against the Dirty Dozen.
The Dirty Dozen formed an alliance with the Hells Angels in Tucson in 1982.
Members of the Mongols, Hells Angels, Straight Satans, and Glendale Night Riders committed a sexual assault together in 1972.
Members of the Mongols, Hells Angels, Straight Satans, and Glendale Night Riders committed a sexual assault together in 1972.
Members of the Hells Angels, Mongols, Straight Satans, and Glendale Night Riders committed a sexual assault together in 1972.
Members of the Hells Angels, Mongols, Straight Satans, and Glendale Night Riders committed a sexual assault together in 1972.
Mongols recruited members from 18th Street gang during the 1990s.
Mongols recruited members from Avenues street gang during the 1990s.
Mongols participated in a narcotics transaction with Bassett Grande in 2004.
A Mongols member was killed after a confrontation with Sangra Sureño in 2004.
Listed as ally in infobox.
